title: Ryan Coogler Wins Best Original Screenplay Oscar for 'Sinners'
text: Ryan Coogler achieved a significant milestone at the 98th Academy Awards, securing his first Oscar win in the Best Original Screenplay category for his vampire horror film "Sinners". This victory came after the film had already accumulated writing accolades from the Critics' Choice, BAFTA, and WGA Awards. Historic Achievement for Horror: The win represents a landmark moment for the horror genre at the Oscars. As the article notes, "this award is particularly special, marking just the second time a horror feature has claimed the category in the show's history." The first instance occurred in 2017 when Jordan Peele's "Get Out" became the inaugural African-American original screenplay winner. Competition and Film Details: Coogler faced formidable competition from Josh Safdie and Ronald Bronstein ("Marty Supreme"), among others. "Sinners" tells the story of twin brothers who return to Mississippi seeking a fresh start, only to encounter supernatural threats that force them into a fight for survival and freedom. Commercial Success: The vampire epic performed impressively at the box office, earning over $370 million and securing the highest opening for an original feature since Peele's "Us". Next Project: Looking forward, Coogler has secured a pilot order from Hulu for a reboot of "The X-Files", where he will write and direct the opening episode, marking his next major venture in television.
